text: Standing before this glass wall, I pause at the threshold between sterile modernity and the vibrant curiosity of the exhibit beyond. The world feels oddly quiet here, save for the distant hum of monitored security and the soft echo of my own footsteps. I am intrigued but reserved, hesitating before I cross into what seems an experimental or creative space. Something about barriers, transparency, and fire hose reels makes me reflect on the nature of protection and curiosity—how boundaries both shelter us and invite us to step through.
